etymology.description: 藕(藕) (ǒu, "lotus root") and 粉(粉) (fěn, "powder; starch") combine to form 藕粉, which refers to the starch derived from the lotus root.
etymology.interpretation: The character 藕(藕) (ǒu, "lotus root") directly indicates the source of the starch, which is the lotus root itself. 粉(粉) (fěn, "powder; starch") conveys the texture and form of the product, which is a finely ground, powdery substance made from processing the lotus root. Together, these characters unmistakably designate a type of starch derived from the lotus root, which is used as a food ingredient for its thickening properties, often in desserts and soups.

examples.chinese: 藕粉(藕粉)是一种中式甜品(是一種中式甜品)。
examples.pinyin: Ǒufěn shì yī zhǒng zhōngshì tiánpǐn.
examples.english: Lotus root starch is a type of Chinese dessert.

examples.chinese: 小时候(小時候)，妈妈常煮藕粉羹(媽媽常煮藕粉羹)。
examples.pinyin: Xiǎoshíhòu, māma cháng zhǔ ǒufěn gēng.
examples.english: When I was young, my mom often cooked lotus root starch soup.

examples.chinese: 藕粉(藕粉)不仅美味(不僅美味)，而且营养丰富(而且營養豐富)。
examples.pinyin: Ǒufěn bùjǐn měiwèi, érqiě yíngyǎng fēngfù.
examples.english: Lotus root starch is not only delicious, but also rich in nutrients.
